master_row.title: Encode Permittivity Relation
master_row.canonical_statement: oint Functors and Natural Transformations} The encoding and decoding operations form an adjoint pair, establishing a fundamental relationship between the blob manifold and the DAANSphere. \begin{theorem}[Encoding-Decoding Adjunction] There exists an adjunction $\langle \varepsilon_{\text{encode}} \dashv \delta_{\text{decode}} \rangle$ with unit $\eta: \text{Id}_{\text{Blob}} \Rightarrow \delta_{\text{decode}} \circ \varepsilon_{\text{encode}}$ and counit $\epsilon: \varepsilon_{\text{encode}} \circ \delta_{\text{decode}} \Rightarrow \text{Id}_{\text{DAAN}}$ satisfying the triangle identities: \begin{align} (\epsilon \circ \varepsilon_{\text{encode}}) \circ (\varepsilon_{\text{encode}} \circ \eta) &= \text{Id}_{\varepsilon_{\text{encode}}}\\ (\delta_{\text{decode}} \circ \epsilon) \circ (\eta \circ \delta_{\text{decode}}) &= \text{Id}_{\delta_{\text{decode}}} \end{align} \end{theorem} This adjunction guarantees that the encoding process preserves essential structural information in a manner that permits faithful reconstruction. The unit and counit morphisms quantify the information loss inherent in the discrete sampling of continuous blob matrices onto the finite set of 153,600 sphe
